title: Window Functions, Analytical Grain, and Time-Aware SQL · Mastery Check
summary: Service leaders need rankings, running effort, previous-event comparisons, and rolling metrics without collapsing detail rows. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a time-aware service-level analytics report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define window partition, order, and frame independently from final result ordering.
Choose ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, and NTILE with explicit tie semantics.
Use LAG, LEAD, FIRST_VALUE, and LAST_VALUE with deliberate ordering and frame behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Time Semantics

quiz case — Compare each ticket with its team's effort and severity peers, retain teams with no resolved work, and rank ties deliberately. Build named query stages, one changed window of 2 rows, and a reconciliation total.
